notes:
Opening opportunity work notes for arb-ex07-20260630025157 plaintiff.

Objective: Plaintiff opening for proposition that by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, Russia and Ukraine had officially reached a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement constituting a general pause, not merely an informal agreement, humanitarian pause, limited sector-specific ceasefire, or future framework.

Record inspected: get_case/list_evidence show five case-packet files, no party-submitted evidence and no prior filings. Read full text of ev_f841be6ff80d_market-page, ev_54e94624d245_primary-evidence, ev_959b165782b3_official-source-record, ev_cf0c77f2fe52_secondary-reporting, and ev_41c18aa39f56_unresolved-record-gaps.

Evidence analysis: Market-page packet states rule and final outcome Yes, but warns Polymarket/context statements are not independent proof. Primary-evidence and secondary-reporting packets summarize preserved source captures: Reuters/Guardian/NPR/CBC/ISW reporting both Russia and Ukraine confirmed a U.S.-brokered May 9-11 ceasefire, reportedly including suspension of all kinetic activity and 1,000-for-1,000 POW exchange. Official-source packet has Kremlin/Ushakov official-adjacent confirmation that Russia accepted Trump initiative concerning ceasefire and prisoner exchange, with key agreement during May 9-11 ceasefire period. Ukrainian side is weaker in direct primary capture: Ukrinform reports Decree No. 374/2026 and Zelenskyy confirmation, but official Ukrainian URL returned 403 in the packet; captured decree is Red Square/parade-specific, while separate reports state a ceasefire regime must be established May 9, 10, 11.

Record tensions/adverse checks: Russian framing ties ceasefire to prisoner exchange/Victory Day; defense can argue humanitarian/limited purpose rather than general pause. Packet records alleged violations/continued hostilities around the period. Unresolved gaps include direct Zelenskyy Telegram/X capture, direct Ukrainian decree, Trump/White House source, battlefield monitoring, official post-period violation/implementation statements, and UMA/Polymarket dispute records.

Native investigation before opening: web search located leads for Kremlin official page en.kremlin.ru/events/president/news/79702; Zelenskyy X status 2052816514051698812; ISW May 8 assessment; Guardian May 8; Moscow Times May 8; ABC News May 8; China Daily May 9; Ukrainska Pravda May 8; Reuters social/video/post references. These remain leads only because opening cannot submit new evidence. Adverse lead snippets include descriptions as temporary/humanitarian and reports of sporadic hostilities/resumption after May 11.

Theory: The rule requires officially reached and publicly announced agreement, not perfect compliance or permanence. Plaintiff should argue record already points to mutual public confirmation of a dated May 9-11 ceasefire and credible-source consensus that it suspended all kinetic activity, making it broader than a sector, Red Square, Black Sea, energy-only, or future-framework arrangement. Plaintiff should acknowledge the principal dispute is scope/character: general pause versus prisoner-exchange/Victory-Day humanitarian/security carveout.

Evidence plan for argument phase: submit accepted source captures for Kremlin page, Zelenskyy X/Telegram or Ukrainian official decree/Ukrinform if primary remains blocked, full Reuters/CBC/Guardian/NPR/ISW captures especially all-kinetic-activity language, and adverse violation/continued-hostility materials. Do not rely on unadmitted outside URLs until accepted as evidence.

Defendant opening work notes, openings:defendant. Reviewed wait_for_opportunity ready payload: final action record_opening_statement only; no evidence admission in opening; text limit 5000, target 3750. Inspected get_case and list_evidence: five case-packet evidence files only; no submitted party evidence. Read full byte ranges for all five packet files.

Issue map: Plaintiff must prove by preponderance that by May 31, 2026 Russia and Ukraine officially reached a publicly announced, mutually agreed halt in military engagement that constituted a general pause, not a limited/humanitarian/sector-specific arrangement. Defense theory: even if there was a public three-day ceasefire label and some mutuality, current record does not yet prove qualifying general pause because official-source materials are narrow and implementation/source-chain gaps remain.

Record analysis: ev_f841be6ff80d market-page sets rule and exclusions; final Yes/platform context not independent proof. ev_54e94624d245 primary-evidence summarizes preserved market facts and source-capture list, with caution that Polymarket context is not proof. ev_959b165782b3 official-source-record preserves Russian Kremlin/Ushakov source framing ceasefire as for prisoner exchange; Ukrainian decree via Ukrinform identified as official decree No. 374/2026 but packet says direct access returned 403 and decree is Red Square/parade targeting, not theater-wide halt. ev_cf0c77f2fe52 secondary-reporting contains affirmative summaries from Reuters/Guardian/NPR/CBC/ISW but also defense leads: Victory Day/Red Square/prisoner-exchange framing, continued violation or retaliation claims, and narrow decree. ev_41c18aa39f56 unresolved-record-gaps identifies missing direct Zelenskyy Telegram/X confirmation, direct Ukrainian decree access, direct Trump/White House/Truth Social source, battlefield monitoring, post-period official implementation/violation statements.

Native investigation before opening: web search queries run: (1) May 8 2026 Russia Ukraine three-day ceasefire May 9 11 prisoner exchange Reuters; (2) site:kremlin.ru 79702 Ushakov ceasefire prisoner exchange May 2026; (3) site:president.gov.ua 374/2026 59389 Zelensky decree Red Square May 2026; (4) ISW May 8 2026 Russia Ukraine agreed three-day ceasefire May 9 11. Found official Kremlin URL https://en.kremlin.ru/events/president/news/79702; search snippet aligns with packet that key agreement was 1,000-for-1,000 exchange during May 9-11 ceasefire period. Found official Ukrainian decree URL now reachable at https://www.president.gov.ua/documents/3742026-59389; web open lines 295-314 show decree title about holding a parade in Moscow, humanitarian purpose in talks with American side, permission to hold May 9 Moscow parade, exclusion of Red Square coordinate box from Ukrainian weapons targeting during parade, effective on signing, signed May 8, 2026. Found ISW May 8 and May 9 pages; May 9 search snippet says Russian and Ukrainian forces continued limited offensive operations across the theater throughout the first day of the ceasefire, potentially important adverse implementation evidence. Attempted curl of Kremlin and ISW pages with UA; command hung/no output and was killed via pkill to preserve filing time.

Opening decision: Do not cite outside URLs as record support in filing because evidence admission is unavailable and sources are not yet AAR evidence. Use outside investigation only to shape source targets and emphasize admitted packet gaps. Opening will concede plaintiff has credible media summaries to investigate but argue burden remains unmet unless later evidence proves general scope and official mutuality with reliable custody; focus council on official-source narrowness, missing direct sources, and implementation evidence.

Merits argument opportunity work notes.

Record refresh: wait_for_opportunity returned arguments:plaintiff. get_case/list_evidence showed no new case-packet files, no prior arguments, two openings present, and original five packet evidence items unchanged before plaintiff submissions. Defendant opening stresses prisoner-exchange/Victory Day framing, Red Square decree narrowness, missing direct Ukrainian and U.S. source captures, and need for general halt proof.

Evidence submissions accepted:
1. ev_209acebc67ff_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-209acebc67ff: ABC News May 8 2026 report. Relevant because it preserves Trump statement that the ceasefire included suspension of all kinetic activity and a 1,000-for-1,000 swap, agreement by Putin and Zelenskyy, Zelenskyy May 9-11 ceasefire-regime language, Kremlin confirmation, and adverse holiday/short-pause context.
2. ev_e2286c9504ec_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-e2286c9504ec: Guardian May 8 2026 report. Corroborates both-side confirmation, May 9-11 dates, all-kinetic-activity language, Zelenskyy Telegram language, Ushakov Russian-side confirmation, and adverse prior/unilateral/violation context.
3. ev_c8434a93386e_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-c8434a93386e: China Daily/Xinhua May 9 2026 report. Corroborates Russia/Ukraine agreement, Ushakov statement, Zelenskyy X language, Trump kinetic-activity statement, Red Square decree limit, and Russian MOD complete halt statement from midnight May 8 to May 10.
4. ev_797c6a2cd3aa_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-797c6a2cd3aa: Ukrainska Pravda May 8 2026 report. Corroborates Russian agreement to extend truce until May 11 and prisoner exchange; contains adverse Victory Day framing.

Additional retrieval notes: attempted curl of Kremlin official page https://en.kremlin.ru/events/president/news/79702 with Mozilla UA; no usable output before timeout/termination. Existing packet official-source record remains available. X/Twitter page opened but returned no readable lines in web open; statement preserved through accepted news extracts and packet summaries, not direct X capture. ISW page fetch returned no readable output / 403-like failure; do not cite as newly submitted evidence, only as packet summary if needed.

Evidence analysis: New evidence directly improves the opening gap by adding public source captures for the U.S. statement, Zelenskyy confirmation, and Russian confirmation. Strongest point is that independent reports preserve the same three elements: agreed May 9-11 dates, mutual confirmation, and all-kinetic-activity language. Defense's strongest point remains that the truce was short and linked to POW exchange/Victory Day/Red Square; answer is that purpose/timing does not negate general operational scope when announced term is suspension of all kinetic activity. Alleged violations and post-period resumption bear on implementation, not whether an official general ceasefire agreement was publicly reached. Direct primary source chain is imperfect, but market rule allows credible-media consensus; multiple independent sources converge and contain adverse context.

Planned filing: offer original market rule, official-source record, secondary-reporting summary, unresolved-gaps/adverse packet, and the four new submitted evidence items. Include technical report search ledger noting direct official/social capture gaps and failed Kremlin/X/ISW attempts. Keep filing under 6000 characters.

Defendant arguments work notes. Opportunity arguments:defendant; final action submit_argument; evidence admission allowed; limits reviewed. Inspected get_case and list_evidence at start. New plaintiff evidence: ev_209acebc67ff ABC, ev_e2286c9504ec Guardian, ev_c8434a93386e China Daily/Xinhua, ev_797c6a2cd3aa Ukrainska Pravda, plus plaintiff technical report. Read exact byte ranges of all four new plaintiff exhibits. They strengthen plaintiff on mutual reporting and the phrase suspension of all kinetic activity, but all are secondary-source extracts and preserve limiting context: Victory Day, prisoner swap, Red Square decree, prior violations, and capture limitations/no direct Telegram/X/Truth Social source.

Defense investigation/source path: carried forward opening search map. Official Ukrainian decree page became accessible at https://www.president.gov.ua/documents/3742026-59389; captured page text showing Decree No. 374/2026 is titled about holding a parade in Moscow, states humanitarian purpose from May 8 talks with American side, permits May 9 Moscow parade, excludes only the territorial square of Red Square from Ukraine's weapons-use plan during the parade, and is signed May 8, 2026. Submitted as ev_4fbef7260120_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-4fbef7260120.

Implementation/adverse source: web search for exact ISW phrase found Critical Threats/ISW May 10, 2026 assessment at https://www.criticalthreats.org/analysis/russian-offensive-campaign-assessment-may-10-2026. Captured relevant text that Russian and Ukrainian forces continued limited offensive operations across the theater throughout the second day of the May 9-11 ceasefire; both sides accused the other of violations; Zelenskyy cited assaults/artillery/drone strikes; Russian MoD alleged Ukrainian violations; NASA FIRMS indicated hostilities decreased but activity did not cease; localized tactical combat persisted; lack of enforcement/monitoring/dispute mechanisms. Submitted as ev_b4bfea1a053a_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-b4bfea1a053a.

Reasoning: Plaintiff likely proves a publicly announced short ceasefire label by media consensus. Defense should not overclaim no mutuality. The winning defense point is scope/qualification: official direct Ukrainian decree is narrow; Russian official source is exchange-purpose framing; the broad 'all kinetic activity' term is mediated mostly through Trump/media extracts, not a direct bilateral instrument; implementation evidence shows theater-wide activity continued. Because the proposition requires a general pause in the conflict and excludes humanitarian/limited arrangements, the more likely characterization is a short Victory Day/prisoner-exchange/parade-security arrangement or at most a nominal ceasefire that did not constitute a general pause.

Planned filing: offer D-1 Ukrainian decree, D-2 ISW/Critical Threats implementation assessment, D-3 official-source packet, D-4 unresolved-gaps packet. Include technical report summarizing source-chain and search limitations. Argument will address council directly and keep distinction between record evidence, investigation, and inference.

Plaintiff rebuttal opportunity work notes.

Record refresh: wait_for_opportunity returned rebuttals:plaintiff. get_case/list_evidence show defendant submitted two new evidence items and a search ledger: ev_4fbef7260120_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-4fbef7260120 (official Ukrainian presidential decree) and ev_b4bfea1a053a_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-b4bfea1a053a (Critical Threats/ISW May 10 implementation assessment). Read full content of both with read_evidence_range.

Opponent evidence analysis:
- D-1 / ev_4fbef... official decree is a direct Ukrainian source. It concerns the Moscow parade, states a humanitarian purpose, permits May 9 Moscow parade, and excludes Red Square coordinate box from Ukraine weapons-use plan for the duration of the parade. It proves a narrow Ukrainian implementation/order regarding Red Square, but does not state it was the only Ukrainian ceasefire commitment. It is consistent with a broader truce plus a specific parade carveout.
- D-2 / ev_b4b... Critical Threats/ISW reports both sides continued limited offensive operations across the theater on the second day of the May 9-11 ceasefire, mutual accusations, Zelenskyy and Russian MOD violation statistics, NASA FIRMS signatures indicating hostilities decreased but did not cease, and continued localized activity. This is helpful defense evidence on implementation. Rebuttal points: it presupposes a May 9-11 ceasefire existed and describes the activity as violations of the ceasefire; it reports decreased hostilities, no large missile packages, only 27 drones during a specified period, some sectors with no ground assaults or no advance; the market question is whether the official arrangement was reached and publicly announced, not perfect compliance; and the exhibit is a May 10 mid-period assessment, not a full post-period adjudication of the agreement.

Decision: no new evidence submission for rebuttal. Existing record and defendant exhibits are sufficient to answer the strongest defense points. Avoid broad second merits argument. Use rebuttal to explain that D-1 does not negate broader agreement, D-2 is breach/implementation evidence rather than agreement-scope evidence, and the credible-media consensus plus all-kinetic activity language remains preponderant. Offer D-1 and D-2 as rebuttal-focused evidence if useful, along with key plaintiff exhibits already offered.

Defendant surrebuttal work notes. Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant; final action submit_surrebuttal or pass; evidence admission technically available but no new evidence appears necessary. Inspected get_case and list_evidence at start. Plaintiff rebuttal introduced no new source evidence, only reoffered/argued existing PX and DX items. Rebuttal's main points: D-1 official Ukrainian decree is narrow but allegedly additive rather than inconsistent with broader truce; D-2 implementation evidence allegedly proves violations/reduced-but-continuing activity, not lack of official agreement; plaintiff says violated ceasefire remains ceasefire and all-kinetic-activity language remains broad.

Analysis: Strongest response is legal/factual classification, not new evidence. Concede that breach alone does not always negate an agreement. But the proposition requires that the official arrangement constituted a general pause, not merely that an agreement label existed. The record's direct official Ukrainian source (D-1) is narrow, humanitarian/parade/Red-Square-specific; the Russian official packet (D-3) is prisoner-exchange-purpose-specific; broad all-kinetic language remains mediated through secondary reporting. D-2 matters to scope/classification because it reports activity did not cease, continued limited operations across the theater, mutual accusations, tactical combat missions, lack of monitoring/enforcement, and only decreased activity. Decreased operational tempo is not a general pause.

No additional source search performed because rebuttal raised no new factual source target; it argued the legal effect of evidence already admitted. Planned surrebuttal: answer plaintiff directly, emphasize plaintiff is collapsing formation into qualification, and ask council to find non-qualifying characterization at least as likely as plaintiff's under preponderance.

Plaintiff closing opportunity work notes.

Record refresh: wait_for_opportunity returned closings:plaintiff. get_case/list_evidence show evidence list stable after rebuttal/surrebuttal; no new evidence after plaintiff rebuttal. Defendant surrebuttal argues plaintiff answered the wrong question and that formation/scope/classification cannot be separated; relies on D-1 direct Ukrainian decree, D-2 continued operations/decreased-but-not-ceased, source-chain gaps, and non-qualifying characterization.

Closing analysis: Emphasize preponderance, not certainty or perfect primary-source chain. PX-1 rule allows credible-media consensus; PX-5/PX-6/PX-7 show mutual official public agreement and all-kinetic-activity language; PX-2/PX-8 corroborate Russian side; Zelenskyy language preserved through accepted reports. Strongest defense point is D-1 direct Ukrainian decree plus D-2 implementation evidence. Response: D-1 is direct but narrow and does not say it is exclusive; D-2 is adverse implementation evidence but repeatedly calls the period a ceasefire and treats operations as violations, and reports decreased tempo/no large missile packages/some no-advance/no-assault details. Short duration, purpose, and violations reduce weight but do not transform an all-kinetic agreed ceasefire into a mere Red Square or humanitarian pause. Concede unresolved gaps: no direct Ukrainian social / U.S. social capture; implementation imperfect; but under market rule and preponderance the credible-media consensus carries. Requested conclusion: proposition true/Yes.

Defendant closing work notes. Opportunity closings:defendant; final action deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence or technical reports permitted. Inspected get_case and list_evidence at start of closing. Plaintiff closing argues PX-1 permits credible-media consensus; PX-5/PX-6/PX-7 converge on all-kinetic activity and mutuality; D-1 is a narrow implementation act not inconsistent with broader ceasefire; D-2 shows violations and reduced-but-continuing activity but not absence of official agreement; source chain imperfect but preponderance satisfied.

Defense closing analysis: Concede plaintiff's strongest facts: credible reporting supports a public three-day ceasefire label, prisoner swap, mutual acknowledgement, and Trump/media all-kinetic wording. But burden is qualifying proposition: official mutual halt constituting general pause, not merely an announced agreement. Decisive defense evidence: D-1 direct official Ukrainian decree is narrow, humanitarian, parade/Red Square-specific; D-3 Russian official packet frames initiative as for prisoner exchange; D-2 implementation evidence shows operations continued across theater, activity decreased but did not cease, tactical combat persisted, and no monitoring/enforcement/dispute mechanisms; D-4 source-chain gaps remain for direct Ukrainian social confirmation, direct U.S. source, and complete implementation materials. Plaintiff tries to classify D-2 as only breach evidence, but for a general-pause proposition, implementation and actual continued operations are probative of scope and whether arrangement constituted a general pause. Closing should ask council to find non-qualifying characterization at least equally likely and therefore plaintiff has not carried preponderance.
